99	## Node Prose
100	
101	### Otto Hightower
102	The King's Hand who took a great dislike to the king's brother Daemon — quote: "took a great dislike to the king’s brother". He convinced Viserys to remove Daemon as master of coin and then master of laws — quote: "to remove Prince Daemon as master of coin". He wrote to his brother, the Lord of Oldtown, that Daemon must never take the throne — quote: "He would be a second Maegor the Cruel, or worse." He then favored Rhaenyra's succession — quote: "than Lord Flea Bottom". In 109 AC Viserys stripped him of his office — quote: "stripped Ser Otto of his chain of office"; he returned to Oldtown but remained loudest among Alicent's supporters — quote: "Loudest amongst her supporters was her father".
103	
104	### Viserys I Targaryen
105	King who hated dissension and strove to keep peace between Daemon and Otto — quote: "King Viserys hated dissension". He announced Aemma with child in 105 AC — quote: "Queen Aemma was once again with child". After Aemma's death he named Rhaenyra his heir — quote: "declared his daughter, Rhaenyra, to be his rightful heir". He wed Alicent Hightower in 106 AC — quote: "took Alicent Hightower to wife in 106 AC". Amiable and easily led, he was not the strongest-willed of kings — quote: "was not the strongest-willed of kings".
106	
107	### Daemon Targaryen
108	The king's brother, Commander of the City Watch with two thousand men — quote: "with two thousand men under his command". Observed in a brothel japing about the dead infant heir — quote: "the “heir for a day.”" He quit the City Watch in fury and took his paramour Mysaria to Dragonstone on Caraxes — quote: "resigning from the City Watch". Ordered back to his wife, he flew to Runestone and his "bronze bitch" — quote: "his “bronze bitch.”" He warred on the Stepstones and slew Craghas Crabfeeder singlehanded — quote: "he slew him singlehanded". He declared himself King of the Stepstones and the Narrow Sea — quote: "declared himself King of the Stepstones and the Narrow Sea". He was later exiled on pain of death — quote: "King Viserys sent him into exile".
109	
110	### Rhaenyra Targaryen
111	Named her father's heir and Princess of Dragonstone — quote: "named her Princess of Dragonstone". Smitten with Ser Criston Cole, whom she called her white knight — quote: "the man she called “my white knight”". She flew daily with her uncle Daemon, racing Syrax against Caraxes — quote: "racing Syrax against Caraxes to Dragonstone and back". Forced to wed, she consented only when threatened with losing the succession — quote: "she consented to be married". In 113 AC she turned sixteen and took Dragonstone as her seat — quote: "took possession of Dragonstone as her own seat". She bore a son, Jacaerys, in the waning days of 114 AC — quote: "Born in the waning days of 114 AC".
112	
113	### Criston Cole
114	A comely knight of three-and-twenty, son of a steward of Lord Dondarrion, appointed to the Kingsguard in 105 AC — quote: "the son of a steward in service to Lord Dondarrion". He won the melee at Maidenpool, knocking Dark Sister from Daemon's hand — quote: "he won the melee held at Maidenpool". He became Rhaenyra's sworn shield and her constant companion — quote: "her own personal shield and protector". Named Lord Commander of the Kingsguard in 112 AC — quote: "named the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard". After she was denied to him his love turned to loathing and he went over to the greens — quote: "turned to loathing and disdain".
115	
116	### Lyonel Strong
117	Lord of Harrenhal, a big burly balding man mistaken for a brute — quote: "burly and balding". He studied at the Citadel, earning six links, and was learned in law — quote: "earning six links of his chain". Thrice-wed and thrice a widower, he brought two daughters and two sons to court — quote: "Thrice-wed and thrice a widower". Viserys made him master of laws, then Hand in 109 AC — quote: "the taciturn Lord of Harrenhal, Lyonel Strong". As Hand he urged that Daemon be put to death as a traitor — quote: "the prince should be put to death immediately as a traitor".
118	
119	### Harwin Strong
120	Eldest son of Lyonel, called Breakbones, made a captain in the gold cloaks — quote: "Ser Harwin Strong, called Breakbones". Heir to Harrenhal and reputedly the strongest man in the realm — quote: "the strongest man in the Seven Kingdoms". He courted Rhaenyra and became her champion after Cole turned against her — quote: "the massive and redoubtable Breakbones". According to Mushroom, it was Harwin who took Rhaenyra's innocence — quote: "It was he who took Rhaenyra’s innocence". Cole broke his collarbone and elbow at the wedding tourney, and Mushroom renamed him Brokenbones — quote: "to name him “Brokenbones” thereafter".
121	
122	### Larys Strong
123	Younger son of Lyonel Strong, called Larys the Clubfoot, who joined the king's confessors — quote: "Larys the Clubfoot, joined the king's confessors".
124	
125	### Aemma Arryn
126	Queen and wife of Viserys, brought to bed in Maegor's Holdfast in late 105 AC — quote: "brought to bed in Maegor’s Holdfast". She died bearing the son Viserys had long desired — quote: "died whilst giving birth to the son".
127	
128	### Mysaria
129	Daemon's paramour, taken to Dragonstone, whom he got with child — quote: "he got Mysaria with child". Daemon gave her a dragon's egg; ordered to send her away, he dispatched her eggless to Lys — quote: "dispatching Mysaria (eggless) back to Lys". She lost her child in a storm on the narrow sea — quote: "Mysaria lost her child during a storm".
130	
131	### Laena Velaryon
132	Daughter of Rhaenys and the Sea Snake, freshly flowered at twelve — quote: "who had just turned twelve". Runciter proposed her as a bride for the king to heal the rift with Driftmark — quote: "the Lady Laena Velaryon". She claimed the dragon Vhagar and cared more for flying than for boys — quote: "Her ladyship shows far more interest in flying than in boys".
133	
134	### Corlys Velaryon
135	The Sea Snake, Lord of the Tides, wealthy as any man in the Seven Kingdoms — quote: "as wealthy and powerful as any man". Suffering most from the Triarchy's tolls, he took Daemon as a war partner against the Stepstones — quote: "in Daemon Targaryen he found a willing partner". He commanded the fleet while Daemon led the army, and crowned Daemon king — quote: "Lord Corlys placed a crown upon his head". He overruled naming Rhaenyra's son Joffrey, giving him the Velaryon name Jacaerys — quote: "overruled by his father, Lord Corlys".
136	
137	### Alicent Hightower
138	Clever, lovely daughter of the King's Hand, eighteen, who had read to the dying Jaehaerys — quote: "the girl who had read to King Jaehaerys as he lay dying". Viserys wed her in 106 AC over Runciter's counsel — quote: "his intention to wed Lady Alicent of House Hightower". She bore Viserys four children and led the queen's party, the greens — quote: "as fertile as she was pretty". Her amity with Rhaenyra proved short-lived as both aspired to be first lady — quote: "both Rhaenyra and Alicent aspired to be the first lady". Some cast doubt on her virtue; according to Mushroom she bedded Viserys before Aemma's death — quote: "cast doubt on Lady Alicent’s virtue".
139	
140	### Laenor Velaryon
141	Rhaenyra's cousin, grandson of Prince Aemon and great-grandson of the Old King — quote: "a grandson of Prince Aemon Targaryen". Nineteen and said to prefer the company of handsome squires — quote: "had never shown any interest in women". Knighted a fortnight before wedding Rhaenyra in 114 AC — quote: "took to husband Ser Laenor Velaryon". He kept his favorite Ser Joffrey Lonmouth, and later Ser Qarl Correy at High Tide — quote: "a household knight named Ser Qarl Correy".
142	
143	### Craghas Drahar
144	Myrish prince admiral who led the Triarchy's conquest of the Stepstones — quote: "the Myrish prince admiral, Craghas Drahar". He earned the sobriquet Crabfeeder by staking captured pirates to drown — quote: "the sobriquet Craghas Crabfeeder". Daemon slew him singlehanded in 108 AC — quote: "he slew him singlehanded and cut off his head".
145	
146	### Joffrey Lonmouth
147	Ser; the Knight of Kisses and Laenor's favorite — quote: "Ser Joffrey Lonmouth, known as the Knight of Kisses". Cole cracked his helm at the wedding tourney and he died six days later — quote: "died without recovering consciousness six days later".
148	
149	### Jacaerys Velaryon
150	Son borne by Rhaenyra late in 114 AC, a large lad with brown hair, brown eyes, and a pug nose — quote: "brown hair, brown eyes, and a pug nose". Given a Velaryon name over Laenor's wish to call him Joffrey — quote: "Velaryon name: Jacaerys". By royal command he shared a wet nurse with the infant Daeron — quote: "shared a wet nurse until weaned".
151	
152	### Daeron Targaryen (son of Viserys)
153	Viserys's third son by Alicent, born as the court celebrated Jacaerys — quote: "delivering Viserys his third son, Daeron". His coloring bespoke his dragon blood, unlike Jace's — quote: "testified to his dragon blood". Raised as a milk-brother to Jacaerys to prevent enmity — quote: "raising them as milk".
154	
155	### Runciter
156	Grand Maester who first urged Viserys to remarry and proposed Laena Velaryon — quote: "the first to urge His Grace to remarry". He reported that the brothers merely quarreled and that Daemon resumed his council seat — quote: "according to Grand Maester Runciter". He died in 112 AC — quote: "Grand Maester Runciter and Ser Harrold Westerling both died in 112 AC".
157	
158	### Mellos
159	Maester sent by the Citadel to take the Grand Maester's chain after Runciter's death — quote: "sent Maester Mellos to the Red Keep". He dismissed the concern over Laenor's tastes — quote: "when fish is served, | eat it".
160	
161	### Johanna Swann
162	A fifteen-year-old niece of the Lord of Stonehelm, enslaved by the Lyseni; when her uncle refused ransom she was sold to a pillow house and rose to become the Black Swan — quote: "the celebrated courtesan known as the Black Swan".

## Node Prose

### Otto Hightower
The King's Hand who took a great dislike to the king's brother Daemon — quote: "took a great dislike to the king’s brother". He convinced Viserys to remove Daemon as master of coin and then master of laws — quote: "to remove Prince Daemon as master of coin". He wrote to his brother, the Lord of Oldtown, that Daemon must never take the throne — quote: "He would be a second Maegor the Cruel, or worse." He then favored Rhaenyra's succession — quote: "than Lord Flea Bottom". In 109 AC Viserys stripped him of his office — quote: "stripped Ser Otto of his chain of office"; he returned to Oldtown but remained loudest among Alicent's supporters — quote: "Loudest amongst her supporters was her father".

### Viserys I Targaryen
King who hated dissension and strove to keep peace between Daemon and Otto — quote: "King Viserys hated dissension". He announced Aemma with child in 105 AC — quote: "Queen Aemma was once again with child". After Aemma's death he named Rhaenyra his heir — quote: "declared his daughter, Rhaenyra, to be his rightful heir". He wed Alicent Hightower in 106 AC — quote: "took Alicent Hightower to wife in 106 AC". Amiable and easily led, he was not the strongest-willed of kings — quote: "was not the strongest-willed of kings".

### Daemon Targaryen
The king's brother, Commander of the City Watch with two thousand men — quote: "with two thousand men under his command". Observed in a brothel japing about the dead infant heir — quote: "the “heir for a day.”" He quit the City Watch in fury and took his paramour Mysaria to Dragonstone on Caraxes — quote: "resigning from the City Watch". Ordered back to his wife, he flew to Runestone and his "bronze bitch" — quote: "his “bronze bitch.”" He warred on the Stepstones and slew Craghas Crabfeeder singlehanded — quote: "he slew him singlehanded". He declared himself King of the Stepstones and the Narrow Sea — quote: "declared himself King of the Stepstones and the Narrow Sea". He was later exiled on pain of death — quote: "King Viserys sent him into exile".

### Rhaenyra Targaryen
Named her father's heir and Princess of Dragonstone — quote: "named her Princess of Dragonstone". Smitten with Ser Criston Cole, whom she called her white knight — quote: "the man she called “my white knight”". She flew daily with her uncle Daemon, racing Syrax against Caraxes — quote: "racing Syrax against Caraxes to Dragonstone and back". Forced to wed, she consented only when threatened with losing the succession — quote: "she consented to be married". In 113 AC she turned sixteen and took Dragonstone as her seat — quote: "took possession of Dragonstone as her own seat". She bore a son, Jacaerys, in the waning days of 114 AC — quote: "Born in the waning days of 114 AC".

### Criston Cole
A comely knight of three-and-twenty, son of a steward of Lord Dondarrion, appointed to the Kingsguard in 105 AC — quote: "the son of a steward in service to Lord Dondarrion". He won the melee at Maidenpool, knocking Dark Sister from Daemon's hand — quote: "he won the melee held at Maidenpool". He became Rhaenyra's sworn shield and her constant companion — quote: "her own personal shield and protector". Named Lord Commander of the Kingsguard in 112 AC — quote: "named the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard". After she was denied to him his love turned to loathing and he went over to the greens — quote: "turned to loathing and disdain".

### Lyonel Strong
Lord of Harrenhal, a big burly balding man mistaken for a brute — quote: "burly and balding". He studied at the Citadel, earning six links, and was learned in law — quote: "earning six links of his chain". Thrice-wed and thrice a widower, he brought two daughters and two sons to court — quote: "Thrice-wed and thrice a widower". Viserys made him master of laws, then Hand in 109 AC — quote: "the taciturn Lord of Harrenhal, Lyonel Strong". As Hand he urged that Daemon be put to death as a traitor — quote: "the prince should be put to death immediately as a traitor".

### Harwin Strong
Eldest son of Lyonel, called Breakbones, made a captain in the gold cloaks — quote: "Ser Harwin Strong, called Breakbones". Heir to Harrenhal and reputedly the strongest man in the realm — quote: "the strongest man in the Seven Kingdoms". He courted Rhaenyra and became her champion after Cole turned against her — quote: "the massive and redoubtable Breakbones". According to Mushroom, it was Harwin who took Rhaenyra's innocence — quote: "It was he who took Rhaenyra’s innocence". Cole broke his collarbone and elbow at the wedding tourney, and Mushroom renamed him Brokenbones — quote: "to name him “Brokenbones” thereafter".

### Larys Strong
Younger son of Lyonel Strong, called Larys the Clubfoot, who joined the king's confessors — quote: "Larys the Clubfoot, joined the king's confessors".

### Aemma Arryn
Queen and wife of Viserys, brought to bed in Maegor's Holdfast in late 105 AC — quote: "brought to bed in Maegor’s Holdfast". She died bearing the son Viserys had long desired — quote: "died whilst giving birth to the son".

### Mysaria
Daemon's paramour, taken to Dragonstone, whom he got with child — quote: "he got Mysaria with child". Daemon gave her a dragon's egg; ordered to send her away, he dispatched her eggless to Lys — quote: "dispatching Mysaria (eggless) back to Lys". She lost her child in a storm on the narrow sea — quote: "Mysaria lost her child during a storm".

### Laena Velaryon
Daughter of Rhaenys and the Sea Snake, freshly flowered at twelve — quote: "who had just turned twelve". Runciter proposed her as a bride for the king to heal the rift with Driftmark — quote: "the Lady Laena Velaryon". She claimed the dragon Vhagar and cared more for flying than for boys — quote: "Her ladyship shows far more interest in flying than in boys".

### Corlys Velaryon
The Sea Snake, Lord of the Tides, wealthy as any man in the Seven Kingdoms — quote: "as wealthy and powerful as any man". Suffering most from the Triarchy's tolls, he took Daemon as a war partner against the Stepstones — quote: "in Daemon Targaryen he found a willing partner". He commanded the fleet while Daemon led the army, and crowned Daemon king — quote: "Lord Corlys placed a crown upon his head". He overruled naming Rhaenyra's son Joffrey, giving him the Velaryon name Jacaerys — quote: "overruled by his father, Lord Corlys".

### Alicent Hightower
Clever, lovely daughter of the King's Hand, eighteen, who had read to the dying Jaehaerys — quote: "the girl who had read to King Jaehaerys as he lay dying". Viserys wed her in 106 AC over Runciter's counsel — quote: "his intention to wed Lady Alicent of House Hightower". She bore Viserys four children and led the queen's party, the greens — quote: "as fertile as she was pretty". Her amity with Rhaenyra proved short-lived as both aspired to be first lady — quote: "both Rhaenyra and Alicent aspired to be the first lady". Some cast doubt on her virtue; according to Mushroom she bedded Viserys before Aemma's death — quote: "cast doubt on Lady Alicent’s virtue".

### Laenor Velaryon
Rhaenyra's cousin, grandson of Prince Aemon and great-grandson of the Old King — quote: "a grandson of Prince Aemon Targaryen". Nineteen and said to prefer the company of handsome squires — quote: "had never shown any interest in women". Knighted a fortnight before wedding Rhaenyra in 114 AC — quote: "took to husband Ser Laenor Velaryon". He kept his favorite Ser Joffrey Lonmouth, and later Ser Qarl Correy at High Tide — quote: "a household knight named Ser Qarl Correy".

### Craghas Drahar
Myrish prince admiral who led the Triarchy's conquest of the Stepstones — quote: "the Myrish prince admiral, Craghas Drahar". He earned the sobriquet Crabfeeder by staking captured pirates to drown — quote: "the sobriquet Craghas Crabfeeder". Daemon slew him singlehanded in 108 AC — quote: "he slew him singlehanded and cut off his head".

### Joffrey Lonmouth
Ser; the Knight of Kisses and Laenor's favorite — quote: "Ser Joffrey Lonmouth, known as the Knight of Kisses". Cole cracked his helm at the wedding tourney and he died six days later — quote: "died without recovering consciousness six days later".

### Jacaerys Velaryon
Son borne by Rhaenyra late in 114 AC, a large lad with brown hair, brown eyes, and a pug nose — quote: "brown hair, brown eyes, and a pug nose". Given a Velaryon name over Laenor's wish to call him Joffrey — quote: "Velaryon name: Jacaerys". By royal command he shared a wet nurse with the infant Daeron — quote: "shared a wet nurse until weaned".

### Daeron Targaryen (son of Viserys)
Viserys's third son by Alicent, born as the court celebrated Jacaerys — quote: "delivering Viserys his third son, Daeron". His coloring bespoke his dragon blood, unlike Jace's — quote: "testified to his dragon blood". Raised as a milk-brother to Jacaerys to prevent enmity — quote: "raising them as milk".

### Runciter
Grand Maester who first urged Viserys to remarry and proposed Laena Velaryon — quote: "the first to urge His Grace to remarry". He reported that the brothers merely quarreled and that Daemon resumed his council seat — quote: "according to Grand Maester Runciter". He died in 112 AC — quote: "Grand Maester Runciter and Ser Harrold Westerling both died in 112 AC".

### Mellos
Maester sent by the Citadel to take the Grand Maester's chain after Runciter's death — quote: "sent Maester Mellos to the Red Keep". He dismissed the concern over Laenor's tastes — quote: "when fish is served, | eat it".

### Johanna Swann
A fifteen-year-old niece of the Lord of Stonehelm, enslaved by the Lyseni; when her uncle refused ransom she was sold to a pillow house and rose to become the Black Swan — quote: "the celebrated courtesan known as the Black Swan".

## Node Prose

### Rhea Royce
The "bronze bitch" of Runestone, Daemon's wife, who fell from her horse while hawking in 115 AC — quote: "fell from her horse whilst hawking and cracked her skull upon a stone". She lingered nine days, then collapsed and died within an hour of rising — quote: "collapse and die within an hour of rising". Her lands passed not to Daemon but to her nephew — quote: "Runestone passed instead to Lady Rhea’s nephew".

### Daemon Targaryen
The king's brother, who flew to the Vale on his wife's death, ostensibly to bury her but hoping for her lands — quote: "To put my wife to rest". Lady Jeyne dismissed his claim and warned him from the Vale — quote: "his presence in the Vale was unwelcome". At High Tide his eye fell on Laena Velaryon; he asked Corlys for her hand and slew her Braavosi betrothed with Dark Sister — quote: "the prince made short work of his rival". He wed Laena and left the Stepstones — quote: "wed Lady Laena Velaryon a fortnight later". Widowed again in 120 AC, he wed his niece Rhaenyra secretly on Dragonstone and at last fathered trueborn sons — quote: "Prince Daemon had at last a living son of his own blood".

### Laena Velaryon
Corlys's daughter, a maid of two-and-twenty, tall and lovely, long betrothed to a wastrel son of the Sealord of Braavos — quote: "betrothed from the age of twelve to a son of the Sealord of Braavos". She wed Daemon and bore him twin daughters in Pentos in 116 AC — quote: "Lady Laena gave birth to twin daughters". In 120 AC she bore a twisted son who died within the hour and succumbed to childbed fever herself at twenty-seven — quote: "making her helpless before the onset of childbed fever". In her final hour she tried to reach Vhagar to fly a last time — quote: "intent on reaching Vhagar that she might fly one last time".

### Rhaenyra Targaryen
The king's heir, who bore Lucerys late in 115 AC and Joffrey in 117 AC — quote: "given birth to a second son late in the year 115 AC". She grew estranged from Queen Alicent and kept to Dragonstone — quote: "the princess spent her days on Dragonstone". Widowed in 120 AC, she wed her uncle Daemon and bore him Aegon the Younger and Viserys — quote: "taking to husband her uncle, Daemon Targaryen". She had Vaemond Velaryon beheaded for calling her sons bastards — quote: "had his head removed, and fed his carcass to her dragon, Syrax". Her healer Gerardys later saved the wounded king's life — quote: "acted swiftly to remove two fingers from His".

### Viserys I Targaryen
The king, who still meant his daughter to follow him and her sons after her — quote: "King Viserys still meant for his daughter to follow him upon the Iron Throne". He forgave Daemon a second time out of love — quote: "the king still loved the brother who had been the companion of his youth". After maiming a hand on the Iron Throne and losing two fingers, he shunned the throne room — quote: "Viserys | never sat upon the Iron Throne again". Grown fat, gouty, and short of breath, he recalled Otto Hightower as Hand — quote: "recalled to court Ser Otto Hightower, the queen’s father". He died in his sleep in 129 AC, aged fifty-two after twenty-six years — quote: "closed his eyes and went to sleep".

### Alicent Hightower
The queen, whose rivalry with Rhaenyra deepened past bearing — quote: "the queen and the princess could scarce suffer each other's presence". According to Mushroom she taunted Laenor over his sons' looks — quote: "soon or late, you may get one who looks like you". After Aemond's maiming she demanded one of Lucerys's eyes — quote: "one of Lucerys Velaryon’s eyes should be put out". She grew wroth at the naming of Rhaenyra's son Aegon and pressed Maester Alfador for Grand Maester — quote: "She urged the appointment of one Maester Alfador".

### Laenor Velaryon
Rhaenyra's husband and the putative father of her children — quote: "the putative father of her children". He was slain at a fair in Spicetown in 120 AC by his companion Ser Qarl Correy — quote: "stabbed to death by his friend and companion Ser Qarl Correy". According to Septon Eustace, jealousy was the motive, Laenor having taken a new favorite — quote: "grown enamored of a new favorite, a handsome young squire of six-and-ten".

### Harwin Strong
Ser, called Breakbones, Rhaenyra's champion and sworn shield, sent back to Harrenhal — quote: "whilst Breakbones returned to Harrenhal". Amongst the greens it was an article of faith he, not Laenor, fathered Rhaenyra's sons — quote: "not her husband, Laenor, but her champion, Harwin Strong". He died with his father in the fire at Harrenhal in 120 AC — quote: "both father and son were killed".

### Lyonel Strong
Lord of Harrenhal and Hand of the King, on whom Viserys relied — quote: "Lord Strong had been the King’s Hand". He returned to Harrenhal with his son and died there when fire broke out in their tower — quote: "a fire broke out in the tower where they were sleeping". The cause was never determined and many blamed foul play — quote: "The cause of the fire was never determined".

### Larys Strong
Larys the Clubfoot, who became Lord of Harrenhal on the deaths of his father and elder brother — quote: "Larys Strong became the Lord of Harrenhal". Some suspected him of the fire; he later sat the small council as Lord Confessor and master of whisperers — quote: "the Lord Confessor and master of whisperers, Larys Strong".

### Aemond Targaryen
Alicent's second son, dragonless at ten, who resolved to claim Vhagar in secret — quote: "he resolved not to wait for Dragonstone". He shoved Joffrey into dragon droppings and mounted Vhagar, becoming a dragonrider — quote: "the boy prince Aemond Targaryen became a dragonrider". In the fight after, he broke Luke's nose and mocked the boys as "the Strongs" before Luke took his eye — quote: "calling them “the Strongs.”". He counted the trade a fair one — quote: "he lost an eye and gained a dragon that day". He grew into a dangerous swordsman under Criston Cole — quote: "under the tutelage of Ser Criston Cole".

### Lucerys Velaryon
Luke, Rhaenyra's brown-haired second son who rode Arrax — quote: "given a dragon's egg whilst in the cradle". In the yard at High Tide he slashed Aemond across the face and took his right eye — quote: "slashed Aemond across the face, taking out his right eye". Rhaenyra later urged Corlys to name him heir to Driftmark over Jacaerys — quote: "urged her good-father to name instead her second son, Lucerys".

### Corlys Velaryon
The Sea Snake, Lord of the Tides, who mourned first his daughter Laena then his son Laenor in 120 AC — quote: "still in mourning for their beloved daughter when the Stranger came again, to carry off their son". He offered ten thousand golden dragons for Laenor's killer — quote: "offered a reward of ten thousand". Stricken by fever in 126 AC, he raised the question of who should succeed him — quote: "the Sea Snake was stricken by a sudden fever".

### Rhaenys
Princess, wife of Corlys and mother of Laena and Laenor, "she who might have been a queen" — quote: "she who might have". She mourned both her children in 120 AC — quote: "still in mourning for their beloved daughter".

### Joffrey Velaryon
Rhaenyra's third son, born 117 AC, brown-haired like his brothers — quote: "brown eyes, brown hair, and features that some at court called “common.”". At three he saw Aemond at Vhagar, was shoved into dung, and ran to fetch his brothers — quote: "Afraid that the boy would raise the alarm". His own young dragon was Tyraxes — quote: "his own young dragon, Tyraxes".

### Qarl Correy
Ser, a household knight of low birth with expensive tastes, who stabbed Laenor at Spicetown and fled, never seen again — quote: "Correy had fled by then". Mushroom holds that Daemon paid him to kill Laenor, then had him killed at sea — quote: "then cut his throat and fed him to the sea".

### Otto Hightower
Ser, the queen's father, recalled to the Handship after Lyonel Strong's death — quote: "recalled to court Ser Otto Hightower, the queen’s father". He remained the King's Hand and led the council as Viserys's strength failed — quote: "The King's Hand remained Ser Otto Hightower".

### Vaemond Velaryon
Ser, eldest of Corlys's nephews, who claimed Driftmark on the grounds that Rhaenyra's sons were bastards — quote: "the inheritance by rights should pass to him". Rhaenyra sent Daemon to seize him and had him beheaded and fed to Syrax — quote: "She dispatched Prince Daemon to seize Ser".

### Aegon the Elder
Alicent's eldest son, wed to his sister Helaena in 122 AC at fifteen, a lazy glutton and lecher — quote: "a glutton at table, given to swilling ale and strongwine". He rode Sunfyre, said the most beautiful dragon ever seen — quote: "Aegon's young Sunfyre was said to be the most beautiful dragon ever seen upon the earth". If Mushroom is believed, he fathered two bastards the year of his twins — quote: "he fathered two bastard children the same year as the twins".

### Helaena Targaryen
Alicent's daughter, a dragonrider of Dreamfyre, wed to her brother Aegon at thirteen — quote: "The bride, his sister, was but thirteen". She bore the twins Jaehaerys and Jaehaera in 123 AC and Maelor in 127 AC — quote: "gave birth to twins, a boy she named Jaehaerys and a girl called Jaehaera".

### Mellos
Grand Maester and moderating council voice, who hinted at the sons' bastardy and mused the king himself might have ordered the Harrenhal fire — quote: "the king himself might have given the command". He washed and bound the king's cut hand, then collapsed and died on the serpentine steps — quote: "Grand Maester Mellos collapsed one night whilst he".

### Gerardys
Maester, Rhaenyra's experienced healer on Dragonstone, who came too late to save Laena — quote: "Maester Gerardys came too late". His swift work removing two of the king's fingers later saved Viserys's life — quote: "it was only his healing skills that had saved the king's life".

### Orwyle
Archmaester chosen by the Conclave to be Grand Maester, thought to favor neither faction — quote: "the Conclave bestowed the chain of office upon Archmaester Orwyle". His potions were held more efficacious than Mellos's leechings — quote: "Orwyle’s potions and tinctures were more efficacious".

### Erryk Cargyll
Ser, of the Kingsguard, appointed to serve as Rhaenyra's sworn shield on Dragonstone — quote: "Ser Erryk Cargyll of the Kingsguard would serve as her sworn shield".

### Daeron Targaryen (son of Viserys I)
Alicent's youngest and most popular son, clever and courteous, who had the dragon Tessarion but had yet to ride — quote: "a lovely blue she-dragon named Tessarion, though he had yet to ride". At twelve in 126 AC he was sent to Oldtown to squire for Lord Hightower — quote: "sent to Oldtown to serve as cupbearer and squire to Lord Hightower".

### Baela Targaryen
Twin daughter of Daemon and Laena, born 116 AC in Pentos, named after Daemon's father — quote: "Baela (after his father)". Betrothed while a toddler to one of Rhaenyra's eldest sons — quote: "the betrothal of her two eldest sons to the daughters of Prince Daemon and Lady Laena".

### Rhaena Targaryen (daughter of Daemon)
Twin daughter of Daemon and Laena, born 116 AC in Pentos, small and sickly but fine-featured — quote: "silver-white hair, and purple eyes".

### Jasper Wylde
Lord, master of laws, called "Ironrod" — by Eustace for his unbending rulings, by Mushroom for another reason — quote: "known amongst the smallfolk as “Ironrod”".

### Tyland Lannister
Ser, the lord admiral and master of ships, brother to the Lord of Casterly Rock — quote: "Ser Tyland Lannister, brother to the Lord of Casterly Rock".

### Lyman Beesbury
The aged Lord, master of coin almost without interruption since the Old King's day — quote: "master of coin, in which capacity he had served almost uninterrupted".

### Criston Cole
Ser, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard and a bitter foe to Rhaenyra, who tutored Aemond in arms — quote: "in him Rhaenyra had a bitter foe".

## Node Prose

### Rhea Royce
The "bronze bitch" of Runestone, Daemon's wife, who fell from her horse while hawking in 115 AC — quote: "fell from her horse whilst hawking and cracked her skull upon a stone". She lingered nine days, then collapsed and died within an hour of rising — quote: "collapse and die within an hour of rising". Her lands passed not to Daemon but to her nephew — quote: "Runestone passed instead to Lady Rhea’s nephew".

### Daemon Targaryen
The king's brother, who flew to the Vale on his wife's death, ostensibly to bury her but hoping for her lands — quote: "To put my wife to rest". Lady Jeyne dismissed his claim and warned him from the Vale — quote: "his presence in the Vale was unwelcome". At High Tide his eye fell on Laena Velaryon; he asked Corlys for her hand and slew her Braavosi betrothed with Dark Sister — quote: "the prince made short work of his rival". He wed Laena and left the Stepstones — quote: "wed Lady Laena Velaryon a fortnight later". Widowed again in 120 AC, he wed his niece Rhaenyra secretly on Dragonstone and at last fathered trueborn sons — quote: "Prince Daemon had at last a living son of his own blood".

### Laena Velaryon
Corlys's daughter, a maid of two-and-twenty, tall and lovely, long betrothed to a wastrel son of the Sealord of Braavos — quote: "betrothed from the age of twelve to a son of the Sealord of Braavos". She wed Daemon and bore him twin daughters in Pentos in 116 AC — quote: "Lady Laena gave birth to twin daughters". In 120 AC she bore a twisted son who died within the hour and succumbed to childbed fever herself at twenty-seven — quote: "making her helpless before the onset of childbed fever". In her final hour she tried to reach Vhagar to fly a last time — quote: "intent on reaching Vhagar that she might fly one last time".

### Rhaenyra Targaryen
The king's heir, who bore Lucerys late in 115 AC and Joffrey in 117 AC — quote: "given birth to a second son late in the year 115 AC". She grew estranged from Queen Alicent and kept to Dragonstone — quote: "the princess spent her days on Dragonstone". Widowed in 120 AC, she wed her uncle Daemon and bore him Aegon the Younger and Viserys — quote: "taking to husband her uncle, Daemon Targaryen". She had Vaemond Velaryon beheaded for calling her sons bastards — quote: "had his head removed, and fed his carcass to her dragon, Syrax". Her healer Gerardys later saved the wounded king's life — quote: "acted swiftly to remove two fingers from His".

### Viserys I Targaryen
The king, who still meant his daughter to follow him and her sons after her — quote: "King Viserys still meant for his daughter to follow him upon the Iron Throne". He forgave Daemon a second time out of love — quote: "the king still loved the brother who had been the companion of his youth". After maiming a hand on the Iron Throne and losing two fingers, he shunned the throne room — quote: "Viserys | never sat upon the Iron Throne again". Grown fat, gouty, and short of breath, he recalled Otto Hightower as Hand — quote: "recalled to court Ser Otto Hightower, the queen’s father". He died in his sleep in 129 AC, aged fifty-two after twenty-six years — quote: "closed his eyes and went to sleep".

### Alicent Hightower
The queen, whose rivalry with Rhaenyra deepened past bearing — quote: "the queen and the princess could scarce suffer each other's presence". According to Mushroom she taunted Laenor over his sons' looks — quote: "soon or late, you may get one who looks like you". After Aemond's maiming she demanded one of Lucerys's eyes — quote: "one of Lucerys Velaryon’s eyes should be put out". She grew wroth at the naming of Rhaenyra's son Aegon and pressed Maester Alfador for Grand Maester — quote: "She urged the appointment of one Maester Alfador".

### Laenor Velaryon
Rhaenyra's husband and the putative father of her children — quote: "the putative father of her children". He was slain at a fair in Spicetown in 120 AC by his companion Ser Qarl Correy — quote: "stabbed to death by his friend and companion Ser Qarl Correy". According to Septon Eustace, jealousy was the motive, Laenor having taken a new favorite — quote: "grown enamored of a new favorite, a handsome young squire of six-and-ten".

### Harwin Strong
Ser, called Breakbones, Rhaenyra's champion and sworn shield, sent back to Harrenhal — quote: "whilst Breakbones returned to Harrenhal". Amongst the greens it was an article of faith he, not Laenor, fathered Rhaenyra's sons — quote: "not her husband, Laenor, but her champion, Harwin Strong". He died with his father in the fire at Harrenhal in 120 AC — quote: "both father and son were killed".

### Lyonel Strong
Lord of Harrenhal and Hand of the King, on whom Viserys relied — quote: "Lord Strong had been the King’s Hand". He returned to Harrenhal with his son and died there when fire broke out in their tower — quote: "a fire broke out in the tower where they were sleeping". The cause was never determined and many blamed foul play — quote: "The cause of the fire was never determined".

### Larys Strong
Larys the Clubfoot, who became Lord of Harrenhal on the deaths of his father and elder brother — quote: "Larys Strong became the Lord of Harrenhal". Some suspected him of the fire; he later sat the small council as Lord Confessor and master of whisperers — quote: "the Lord Confessor and master of whisperers, Larys Strong".

### Aemond Targaryen
Alicent's second son, dragonless at ten, who resolved to claim Vhagar in secret — quote: "he resolved not to wait for Dragonstone". He shoved Joffrey into dragon droppings and mounted Vhagar, becoming a dragonrider — quote: "the boy prince Aemond Targaryen became a dragonrider". In the fight after, he broke Luke's nose and mocked the boys as "the Strongs" before Luke took his eye — quote: "calling them “the Strongs.”". He counted the trade a fair one — quote: "he lost an eye and gained a dragon that day". He grew into a dangerous swordsman under Criston Cole — quote: "under the tutelage of Ser Criston Cole".

### Lucerys Velaryon
Luke, Rhaenyra's brown-haired second son who rode Arrax — quote: "given a dragon's egg whilst in the cradle". In the yard at High Tide he slashed Aemond across the face and took his right eye — quote: "slashed Aemond across the face, taking out his right eye". Rhaenyra later urged Corlys to name him heir to Driftmark over Jacaerys — quote: "urged her good-father to name instead her second son, Lucerys".

### Corlys Velaryon
The Sea Snake, Lord of the Tides, who mourned first his daughter Laena then his son Laenor in 120 AC — quote: "still in mourning for their beloved daughter when the Stranger came again, to carry off their son". He offered ten thousand golden dragons for Laenor's killer — quote: "offered a reward of ten thousand". Stricken by fever in 126 AC, he raised the question of who should succeed him — quote: "the Sea Snake was stricken by a sudden fever".

### Rhaenys
Princess, wife of Corlys and mother of Laena and Laenor, "she who might have been a queen" — quote: "she who might have". She mourned both her children in 120 AC — quote: "still in mourning for their beloved daughter".

### Joffrey Velaryon
Rhaenyra's third son, born 117 AC, brown-haired like his brothers — quote: "brown eyes, brown hair, and features that some at court called “common.”". At three he saw Aemond at Vhagar, was shoved into dung, and ran to fetch his brothers — quote: "Afraid that the boy would raise the alarm". His own young dragon was Tyraxes — quote: "his own young dragon, Tyraxes".

### Qarl Correy
Ser, a household knight of low birth with expensive tastes, who stabbed Laenor at Spicetown and fled, never seen again — quote: "Correy had fled by then". Mushroom holds that Daemon paid him to kill Laenor, then had him killed at sea — quote: "then cut his throat and fed him to the sea".

### Otto Hightower
Ser, the queen's father, recalled to the Handship after Lyonel Strong's death — quote: "recalled to court Ser Otto Hightower, the queen’s father". He remained the King's Hand and led the council as Viserys's strength failed — quote: "The King's Hand remained Ser Otto Hightower".

### Vaemond Velaryon
Ser, eldest of Corlys's nephews, who claimed Driftmark on the grounds that Rhaenyra's sons were bastards — quote: "the inheritance by rights should pass to him". Rhaenyra sent Daemon to seize him and had him beheaded and fed to Syrax — quote: "She dispatched Prince Daemon to seize Ser".

### Aegon the Elder
Alicent's eldest son, wed to his sister Helaena in 122 AC at fifteen, a lazy glutton and lecher — quote: "a glutton at table, given to swilling ale and strongwine". He rode Sunfyre, said the most beautiful dragon ever seen — quote: "Aegon's young Sunfyre was said to be the most beautiful dragon ever seen upon the earth". If Mushroom is believed, he fathered two bastards the year of his twins — quote: "he fathered two bastard children the same year as the twins".

### Helaena Targaryen
Alicent's daughter, a dragonrider of Dreamfyre, wed to her brother Aegon at thirteen — quote: "The bride, his sister, was but thirteen". She bore the twins Jaehaerys and Jaehaera in 123 AC and Maelor in 127 AC — quote: "gave birth to twins, a boy she named Jaehaerys and a girl called Jaehaera".

### Mellos
Grand Maester and moderating council voice, who hinted at the sons' bastardy and mused the king himself might have ordered the Harrenhal fire — quote: "the king himself might have given the command". He washed and bound the king's cut hand, then collapsed and died on the serpentine steps — quote: "Grand Maester Mellos collapsed one night whilst he".

### Gerardys
Maester, Rhaenyra's experienced healer on Dragonstone, who came too late to save Laena — quote: "Maester Gerardys came too late". His swift work removing two of the king's fingers later saved Viserys's life — quote: "it was only his healing skills that had saved the king's life".

### Orwyle
Archmaester chosen by the Conclave to be Grand Maester, thought to favor neither faction — quote: "the Conclave bestowed the chain of office upon Archmaester Orwyle". His potions were held more efficacious than Mellos's leechings — quote: "Orwyle’s potions and tinctures were more efficacious".

### Erryk Cargyll
Ser, of the Kingsguard, appointed to serve as Rhaenyra's sworn shield on Dragonstone — quote: "Ser Erryk Cargyll of the Kingsguard would serve as her sworn shield".

### Daeron Targaryen (son of Viserys I)
Alicent's youngest and most popular son, clever and courteous, who had the dragon Tessarion but had yet to ride — quote: "a lovely blue she-dragon named Tessarion, though he had yet to ride". At twelve in 126 AC he was sent to Oldtown to squire for Lord Hightower — quote: "sent to Oldtown to serve as cupbearer and squire to Lord Hightower".

### Baela Targaryen
Twin daughter of Daemon and Laena, born 116 AC in Pentos, named after Daemon's father — quote: "Baela (after his father)". Betrothed while a toddler to one of Rhaenyra's eldest sons — quote: "the betrothal of her two eldest sons to the daughters of Prince Daemon and Lady Laena".

### Rhaena Targaryen (daughter of Daemon)
Twin daughter of Daemon and Laena, born 116 AC in Pentos, small and sickly but fine-featured — quote: "silver-white hair, and purple eyes".

### Jasper Wylde
Lord, master of laws, called "Ironrod" — by Eustace for his unbending rulings, by Mushroom for another reason — quote: "known amongst the smallfolk as “Ironrod”".

### Tyland Lannister
Ser, the lord admiral and master of ships, brother to the Lord of Casterly Rock — quote: "Ser Tyland Lannister, brother to the Lord of Casterly Rock".

### Lyman Beesbury
The aged Lord, master of coin almost without interruption since the Old King's day — quote: "master of coin, in which capacity he had served almost uninterrupted".

### Criston Cole
Ser, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard and a bitter foe to Rhaenyra, who tutored Aemond in arms — quote: "in him Rhaenyra had a bitter foe".

Done. Wrote the enrichment proposal to `/Users/mnoth/source/asoiaf-chat/extractions/fire-and-blood/fab-heirs-of-the-dragon-15-p03.enrichment.md`.

Coverage summary of this unit (115–129 AC, the run-up to the Dance):

- **Entity Roster** — ~95 named entities, with mandatory disambiguators on every same-name-prone one. Notably split the two Aegons per the text's own footnote (Aegon the Elder = Alicent's son; Aegon the Younger = Rhaenyra & Daemon's son), and the two Rhaenas (Daemon's twin daughter vs. Maegor's "Black Bride"), the two Jaehaeryses (Aegon's twin son vs. the Old King namesake), and the two Viseryses.
- **Node Prose** — full 2–5 sentence blocks for the ~20 principals (Daemon, Laena, Rhaenyra, Viserys I, Alicent, Laenor, Harwin, the Strongs, Aemond, Corlys, etc.), one-liners for minor council members, roster-only for pure mentions.
- **Relationships** — LOCKED-vocab edges only; dragon bonds as `BONDED_TO`, kinship/succession/conflict edges, semantic-agent in Column A (e.g. `Rhaenyra EXECUTES Vaemond`, with `Daemon CAPTURES Vaemond` as the instrument).
- **Events** — 26 candidate event nodes with AC years only where the text states them.

**Provenance handling** — the disputed/attributed texture is tagged, not smoothed:
- Paternity of Rhaenyra's sons → both accounts emitted: `Laenor PARENT_OF (claimed)` (eustace) *and* `Harwin PARENT_OF (rumored)` (gyldayn-synthesis, since Gyldayn explicitly weighs Mushroom/Mellos/Eustace).
- Laenor's murder → the killing by Qarl Correy narrated flatly; the `Daemon CONTRACTED_WITH/KILLS Qarl` theory tagged `mushroom, disputed`.
- Harrenhal fire → all four blame-theories emitted as separate disputed `KILLS` rows (Corlys/mushroom, Daemon/eustace, Larys/unattributed, Viserys/mellos).
- Plus Mushroom/Eustace-attributed items (Alicent's taunt, Rhaenyra's resentment/vigil, Aegon's bastards, the Valyria trip) tagged and left off plain narration.

Verbatim quotes were copied with OCR garbles intact (e.g. `Viserys |`), canonical spellings kept to the roster note column. `first_available: pre-agot` mirrored verbatim. The harvest sidecar was not present, so that section was omitted (matching the p01/p02 convention).

One judgment call to flag: the `Jacaerys→Vermax` / `Lucerys→Arrax` dragon pairings rest on narrative order (only `Joffrey→Tyraxes` is stated outright); the betrothal pairing of which twin to which son is left unpaired since the text doesn't specify.
